_about: A recorded, representative Transcript Analyzer output for the sample transcript, used to score the eval offline when no OPENAI_API_KEY is set (CI). It is deliberately imperfect — it folds the audit-log concern into the rotation one, misses the cost concern, and misses the Terraform action item — so the eval reports a meaningful, non-perfect score. With a key set, src/eval.py scores a live analyzer run instead of this.

technical_concerns.concern: Secrets are sprawled across Kubernetes secrets, a 1Password vault, and environment variables baked into images
Database credentials are not rotated and there is no central audit log of access
technical_concerns.context_quote: no rotation on database credentials, and no central audit log of who accessed what

technical_concerns.concern: App teams may not adopt Vault given the integration effort
technical_concerns.context_quote: we spend three months and the app teams never adopt it

technical_concerns.concern: Running Vault self-managed is an operational burden for a small team
technical_concerns.context_quote: I don't want to be paged at 2am because Vault sealed itself

technical_concerns.concern: Audit logs need to land cleanly in the Splunk SIEM
technical_concerns.context_quote: our SIEM is Splunk and it has to land there cleanly

action_items.action: Prepare a cost comparison of HCP Vault managed versus self-hosted
Share a Splunk integration example for Vault audit logs
Send a recap email with next steps by end of week

key_quotes: This was helpful — feeling good about the direction.
Budget is tight this year, so the managed-versus-self-hosted cost difference matters a lot.
